summary: The premium Rebel featuring Penta TX canopy material for lighter weight and faster response. Delivers even more explosive lift and extended hangtime compared to the standard Rebel.

sources.summary: Riders note the Penta TX canopy makes a noticeable difference in responsiveness and weight savings over the standard Rebel.
Early adopters report noticeably quicker turning and better drift compared to the standard version.
Praised as the ultimate big air kite with the Penta TX material delivering tangible performance gains.

reviews.video_title: Big Air Kite Review 16 - Rebel D/Lab
reviews.excerpt: hello and welcome to part two of my Duo te Rebel range comparison please check out part one if you haven't already where I discussed my time on the EVO dlabs and then start this comparison by covering the last couple versions of the Rebel SLS kites also very quickly if you like these reviews and want to contribute to me being able to make more you
reviews.verdict: in summary I really enjoyed all of these Rebels and would likely take them over a lot of the other Big Air Kites on the market right now but thankfully for my wallet they are not quite enough to make me want to jump away from my evos just yet if I didn't already have these evos or they got damaged so much that I would need to replace them I would definitely still get another 10 or 12 M Evo D laab I believe and in the 8 m size I'm not 100% sure but I might actually go for the 8m rebel SLS uh over the rebel DB mainly because of the cost difference but also the potential resale value and its life
reviews.full_transcript: hello and welcome to part two of my Duo te Rebel range comparison please check out part one if you haven't already where I discussed my time on the EVO dlabs and then start this comparison by covering the last couple versions of the Rebel SLS kites also very quickly if you like these reviews and want to contribute to me being able to make more you can follow the link in the description to send me a little tip on the give a little page all donated funds go right back into the reviews of course and once again don't forget to check out Matt kite boarding to get all of your delicious duone gear now let's get into the good stuff and take a look at what seems to be getting marketed as the new flagship performance Big Air Kite from duone the rebel daab of course after talking about the ebos and the rebel SLS this is where things start to get pretty interesting I should first off start by mentioning that it would appear duone have put quite a bit of work to make this kite more durable than the evos as the kite feels a little bit heavier certainly more robust and you are now recommended to inflate them up to P 10 psi this added weight like the north Ultra does come at a degree of compromise to the potential performance of course but I can start by saying right out of the gate that I did overall find the daab version of the Rebel an improvement and more enjoyable than the SLS but to what degree and in what areas is where things get a little tricky the alula material is of course still alula so as I mentioned in part one what kind of shelf life it will have is hard to say potentially Beyond a year and a half as I already know mine have lasted only time will tell on that front onto the flight characteristics first off the bar pressure actually felt marginally heavier or at least what you might call firmer than the 2025 SLS but I would say this was more due to its improved responsiveness and your ability to feel more of the kite all at once due to the rigidity from the alula through the frame I noticed this with the north Ultra as well there was added power but the overall ability to feel more of the kite makes it hard to determine how much more bar pressure is from that or just being harder to move the kite around of course it was certainly within the margin of error from the SLS to the daab and not as noticeable as say the bar pressure increase from the orbit Pro to orbit Ultra moving the kite around the window was certainly more direct and I'd say the kite was also faster through the window but it certainly didn't feel as quick or direct as my evos by comparison the power felt improved but still fairly similar to the SLS and on the session I rode them both together back to back although I generally felt I was more powered and getting more lift or at least Hang Time on the DB I actually jumped higher that session on the SLS but of course the wind is never the same and the jumps were all within 10% of each other on both woo and Surfer the daab version would certainly get rewarded more often than the SLS with random gusts giving you little boosts and bonus lift and and of course that also translated into a more impressive hangtime but once again I don't think either of these areas are any better than my evos although there's perhaps an argument for the hang time on the rebel um and because the Evo tends to move quicker I would wager that a more experienced rider could actually jump higher on the EVO where the rebel is superior though is that it definitely has a larger sweet spot when you do go to send it so although you might not have the same single jump or top few jumps in a session on average most Riders I would wager would jump higher on Rebels to that point I've actually noticed another local Rider here who has just updated his Evo daab quiver to Rebel DBS in the last few months and this tends to be what happens with him on average he seems to be jumping a bit higher and more consistently high but the highest jumps in and his sessions don't seem to be getting quite the same level he would occasionally get when he was back on his Evo DBS he does seem to be enjoying the new Rebels more however because it would appear like he tends to feel a bit more control over them overall even though it's taken a little getting used to the increased Yank on the loops he is at the level where he regularly jumps late teens and early 20s and does standard kite Loops both ways but is a bit older than me and not particularly interested in progressing kite Loop rotations or variations so I would say he is at the ideal level for uh people moving to the rebel dlab that won't be held back perhaps from progressing on them in terms of looping again the daab seems very similar to the SLS in that the kite is a bit slower to the top Loopers on the market and has a ton of yank in the loops I felt like the daab version engaged faster when you're going to send the kite but actually had more yank than the SLS likely a bit from The increased power but also in the rigidity in the frame where the kite won't be able to turn as quickly on itself the SLS seems to have a bit more give so you can crank bar um for a slightly softer Loop whereas the daab there wasn't really much give it all in the kite as he piloted it through the window I would imagine this is because the SLS of course is a bit softer material that contorts more on itself so when the kite is going around the loop with a harder pull it will give a bit more whereas the DB's rigidity seems to hold it shape better which is of course good for jumping and hang time but doesn't feel as friendly on the loops I did feel this kite was very well balanced between the bar pressure and its power through the loop however so I felt like I did know where the kite was a bit more than with the SLS and and was able to pluck up the courage to try some rotations just for this edit really that's something it took me a lot longer to get the confidence for on my evos but I would have to say this cage also came from another year of progressing with all kinds of different kites as well and as you can see from this clip here I have gotten pretty comfortable looping my 10m Evo with rotations sty as well one somewhat annoying uh aspect I've found with lu this Rebel is that in the longer sessions with more wind both myself and my mate um I just mentioned have been noticing the joints in our elbows tend to get a little bit sore and tight around the two to two and a half hour mark something I can't recall being an issue in the past year or so with my evos or ever on my orbits in the past but I do recall getting similar issues with my old XR 7s I'm hoping that with time time you would get used to the power and pull of the kite and the wear it puts on your joints and this would stop being an issue but if you're getting a bit older like we are and have some joint pain already in that area it's something to consider if you also like riding for longer lit sessions and doing a lot of Loops it is interesting to see the duone Big Air Team Riders swapping to the rebel because I don't find that it quite has the same overall performance as the Evo I would chalk this up to either pressure from the brand or potentially if you consider some of the recent Trend and judging that I spoke about in the NXT review that is somewhat leaning towards Big single technical Loops again with more height so it does make sense because the rebel would offer those Riders a bigger sweet spot to boost High similar lift and hangtime to the EVO to get their tricks in and most likely a lower average kite angle from just the slower looping speed of the kite I'm fairly confident you won't be seeing many double loops on this Rebel DB so I guess they we'll have to go back to the evos if those tend to be scoring a lot higher in King of the air this year for your average Rider I think the rebel SLS or DB in sizes 78 and perhaps 9 would be an easy ride for straight boosting um compared to the Evo dlabs but perhaps not as easy for progress into kite Loops as with the Evo um neither these Rebels or evos would probably be my picks for your first kite Loops of course when compared to other kites like the orbits or the recent Thrive from Harlem in sizes T and up however it's hard for me to imagine too many advantages in any of the rebels over the evos because they don't offer the same low end they're heavier to use and don't really offer too much if any more lift or hang time with the five struts they would be a little bit more stable in big winds in any respective size of course but the Evo if still in good condition is already exceptionally stable for a three strut kite due to the alula so it's not a huge difference or Advantage there in my opinion either so in summary I really enjoyed all of these Rebels and would likely take them over a lot of the other Big Air Kites on the market right now but thankfully for my wallet they are not quite enough to make me want to jump away from my evos just yet if I didn't already have these evos or they got damaged so much that I would need to replace them I would definitely still get another 10 or 12 M Evo D laab I believe and in the 8 m size I'm not 100% sure but I might actually go for the 8m rebel SLS uh over the rebel DB mainly because of the cost difference but also the potential resale value and its lifespan even though I do prefer the overall experience of the Rebel dlab over the rebel SLS aside from the loops I think this of course is quite subjective and with more time on both kites I might just have to Fork out the extra cash for the daab version of the Rebel with further testing my gut tells me I'd be even more inclined to stick with the dlab rebel if I had a few more sessions on it in the 35 to 40 knot or more wind range as with my recent experiences when I was testing the orbits and my discovered passion for the ultra in the new king winds my ongoing fear with Lula now after a year on it is that I've also noticed that trying to sell alula kites to the secondhand Market is very challenging uh at least here in our very small market and you can't really blame people because the jury is still out on how well they will age and we all know dakron ages very well and is generally easier to prepare that said once you've gotten used to riding alula kites it is really really hard to go back I must say so thank you all once again for making it this far please like And subscribe for algorithm goodness and fire some comments below with your experiences on any of these kites um and of course what else you would like me to get on to next if you could also check out my Instagram you'll be able to see a lot more Clips around New Zealand and keep up with what other kites I am working on so until next time goodbye [Music]
